title: TRANSITIONAL LIVING PROGRAM - 211info
text: COMMUNITY WORKS TRANSITIONAL LIVING PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a two year transitional living program. Homes are located throughout Jackson County. Offers comprehensive case management, life skills classes, support in finding employment, and awareness of educational opportunities. 2594 E Barnett Road Suite C Medford, OR 97504 Eligibility: Individuals age 18-23 who are homeless or at risk for homelessness Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 779-4357 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Helpline (541) 779-4357 Español (541) 779-2393 ext 209 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for more information FEES: No fees for supportive services, but subsidized housing rates are adjusted by income DOCUMENTS: Completed TLP application, identification, verification of housing status LANGUAGES: English, interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: Jackson County SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 8:30am-4pm TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Location is in the RE/MAX building, in a complex on the corner of E Barnett Road and Black Oak Drive. Use the RE/MAX entrance and take stairs or elevator to the lower floor. Services: Case/Care Management * Homeless Youth Transitional Housing/Shelter * Homeless Youth Other Services Offered At This Location: HOUSING/SHELTER - JACKSON COUNTY CONTINUUM OF CARE SCREENINGS INFORMATION SERVICES - DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SURVIVOR ADVOCACY INFORMATION SERVICES - LABOR AND SEX TRAFFICKING SURVIVOR ADVOCACY INFORMATION SERVICES - SEXUAL ASSAULT SURVIVOR ADVOCACY MENTAL HEALTH ASSESSMENT AND TREATMENT - HELPLINE If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. COMMUNITY WORKS
